seo.page_title: Audi S4 Avant B9 Import NZ
seo.meta_description: Import an Audi S4 Avant B9 from Japan with clear landed pricing, auction documents before bidding, and NZ-based support since 2009.
seo.h1_tag: Import an Audi S4 Avant B9
seo.og_title: Audi S4 Avant B9 Import to NZ
seo.og_description: See how importing an Audi S4 Avant B9 from Japan works, what landed cost covers, and what to check before bidding in NZ.

Audi S4 Avant B9 landed cost NZ

blocks.hero_h1: Import an Audi S4 Avant B9
blocks.hero_subtitle: The Audi S4 Avant B9 gives you wagon practicality with S-badge performance in one package. We source from Japanese auctions, show you the documentation before bidding, and map out the landed cost for NZ from the start.
proof_points: Importing Estimate — compliance is part of the process
blocks.why_kiwis_buy: The S4 Avant B9 suits buyers who want one car to cover the weekday commute, family hauling, and open-road trips. The wagon body is easier to live with than a sedan, while Japanese auction supply can open up more spec and condition options for NZ buyers.

savings_block.headline: Where the money goes — auction purchase through to NZ landed cost
savings_block.body: Because this page has no fixed price range in the source data, we quote each S4 Avant B9 individually rather than guessing. Your total typically starts with the Japanese auction purchase price, then adds shipping, marine insurance, NZ port charges, NZ entry certification, compliance, and our fee so you can see the full landed figure before moving ahead.

cta_form.headline: Find your Audi S4 Avant B9 — we'll do the searching
cta_form.subtext: Fill in your details to get started. We'll search the Japanese auctions and come back with real options and pricing by email.

blocks.video_caption: Watch for the long-roof shape and low stance as the car moves. It shows why the S4 Avant B9 stands out as a wagon that still looks sharp from every angle.
blocks.main_usp_deep: The main draw of the Audi S4 Avant B9 is that it blends performance and usable space in a way few wagons do. For NZ buyers, that means one vehicle that can handle commuting, luggage, and longer trips without feeling like a compromise. Japanese auction sourcing also gives you a better shot at finding the colour, trim, and condition you actually want. We focus on documented examples and flag any noted issues before you commit.

buyer_persona.headline: Who is this car right for?
buyer_persona.body: This suits buyers who want a premium wagon rather than an SUV, but still need room for people and gear. It's also a good option for someone stepping out of a hot hatch or sedan and wanting more practicality without losing the appeal of a performance model. If that sounds like you, it is a solid match.

trust_company.headline: Why import with ImportYourCar.co.nz?
trust_company.body: We've been importing vehicles for NZ buyers since 2009 and have handled more than 1000 cars over that time. Our team is NZ-based, we specialise in Japanese auction sourcing, and we provide the auction sheet and supporting documents before bidding. We keep the process clear, explain the likely landed cost up front, and help guide the car through NZ entry certification and compliance once it arrives.

car_specs_benefits.benefit: Gives you access to a wider pool of S4 Avant B9 examples than waiting on the local market alone.
More practical for luggage, prams, tools, or sports gear than a comparable sedan.
Works for daily family use while still keeping the car compact enough for normal NZ parking and commuting.
Suits buyers who want stronger road presence and a more engaging drive than a standard wagon.
Lets you choose based on auction grade, condition notes, and spec rather than buying whatever happens to be available locally.

standout_features_block.headline: What sets it apart
standout_features_block.intro: On the right Japanese-market example, these are the features that make the S4 Avant B9 more appealing to live with in NZ.

items.title: Audi virtual cockpit
items.body: Many Japanese-market S4 Avant B9 cars were optioned with Audi's digital instrument display. It puts navigation and driving information directly in front of the driver, which is handy on longer NZ trips and keeps the cabin feeling more current than older analogue layouts.
items.image_hint: Driver's view of the digital instrument cluster lit up with navigation map while parked

items.title: Quattro all-wheel drive
items.body: The S4 Avant B9's all-wheel-drive setup is a big part of its appeal for mixed NZ conditions. It gives the car a more planted feel in wet weather and on uneven rural roads, while still keeping the wagon format practical for everyday use.
items.image_hint: Audi S4 Avant driving on a wet winding road with the wagon body clearly visible

items.title: Driver-assist package options
items.body: Depending on the Japanese-market spec, you may find adaptive cruise control, lane support, and related safety tech. These features matter on long motorway runs and can make the car easier to live with if you want performance without giving up modern driver assistance.
items.image_hint: Steering wheel and instrument display showing adaptive cruise active on an open road

items.title: Hands-free wagon practicality
items.body: Higher-spec examples can include convenience features such as a powered tailgate, which suits a wagon used for family gear or travel bags. It's a small thing, but it adds real day-to-day value when you're loading up in tight car parks or in the rain.
items.image_hint: Rear tailgate open with luggage being loaded into the wagon boot at a car park

cta_mid_page.headline: Want us to shortlist a few good examples?
cta_mid_page.subtext: Send us your preferred spec and budget, and we'll check current Japanese auction stock.

models_comparison.intro: If you're looking at an S4 Avant B9, these are the main things to compare between Japanese auction examples.

rows.notes: Year affects equipment, auction availability, and NZ entry requirements. We check each car against the relevant NZ standards for its build year.
This page's source data does not list exact powertrain variants, so we confirm the Japanese-market setup on each shortlisted car before bidding.
Equipment can vary by year and option pack, including driver-assist tech, digital displays, and interior trim. Auction sheets and photos help verify what is actually fitted.
Entry outcomes depend on the individual vehicle, its documentation, and any noted modifications. Based on auction documentation, this model usually meets NZ entry standards if documentation is clean.
Availability changes week to week. Better colour and spec choice usually comes from being flexible on timing rather than rushing the first listing.

steps.title: Tell us what you're after
steps.body: Fill in the form or message us. We confirm your brief and give you a cost breakdown before anything moves forward.

steps.title: We search the auctions
steps.body: We monitor USS, TAA, HAA and others. For a model like the S4 Avant B9, we flag any modification notes on the auction sheet before shortlisting.

steps.title: You approve, we bid
steps.body: We send you the auction sheet and our recommendation. Once you're happy, you pay a deposit and we bid on your behalf.

steps.title: Shipping to NZ
steps.body: Typically 2–4 weeks depending on the sailing schedule. For less common models, allow a little extra time once the auction clears. We keep you updated along the way.

steps.title: Compliance and delivery
steps.body: The car goes through NZ entry certification and compliance. Once it's through, we arrange delivery or you can collect.

risks_solutions.intro: Importing is straightforward, but it helps to know the main factors before you bid.

items.solution: We review auction sheets, photos, and notes carefully and point out any listed changes before you decide whether to bid.
We break down the likely landed figure into auction purchase, shipping, insurance, NZ port charges, NZ entry certification, compliance, and our fee so the numbers are clearer from the start.
Auction grades are useful, but they are only part of the picture. We provide the auction documentation and explain what the notes mean in plain language.

blocks.how_to_use_iyc: Tell us the S4 Avant B9 spec, colour, and budget you want, and we'll search the Japanese auctions for matching cars. We shortlist the better options, explain the paperwork, and quote the likely landed cost before you commit. If you want to see what's available now, send us your brief and we'll take a look.

faq_block.q: Why import an Audi S4 Avant B9 from Japan instead of waiting locally?
faq_block.a: Japan can offer more choice on spec, colour, and condition than the NZ used market at any given time. It also means you can buy based on auction documents rather than guessing from a short local listing.

faq_block.q: What does the landed cost usually include?
faq_block.a: It typically includes the auction purchase price, shipping, marine insurance, NZ port charges, NZ entry certification, compliance, and our fee. We quote this as a full package before you move ahead.

faq_block.q: Will an imported S4 Avant B9 meet NZ standards?
faq_block.a: Based on auction documentation, this model usually meets NZ entry standards if documentation is clean. Final entry and compliance outcomes depend on the individual vehicle and its condition once inspected in NZ.

faq_block.q: How long does the import process take?
faq_block.a: After a successful auction purchase, shipping is typically 2–4 weeks depending on vessel timing. Allow extra time for sourcing, port processing, and compliance in NZ.

faq_block.q: How do I know what condition the auction car is in?
faq_block.a: We provide the auction sheet and available photos before bidding. That gives you a clearer view of grade, interior and exterior notes, and any listed issues.

footer_cta.headline: Ready to look for the right S4 Avant B9?
footer_cta.subtext: We can check current Japanese auction stock, explain the paperwork, and map out the likely landed cost for NZ. Send us your brief and we'll come back with real options.

subscription_block.headline: Stay in the loop
subscription_block.subtext: Get auction alerts, import updates, and practical guides for buying from Japan.

blocks.company_positioning: ImportYourCar.co.nz is an NZ-based Japanese vehicle import company that has been operating since 2009. We specialise in sourcing vehicles from Japanese auctions, providing the documentation before bidding, and guiding buyers through the import process from purchase to NZ arrival.

landed_cost_breakdown.intro: Your landed cost is the full chain from buying in Japan through to getting the car ready for the NZ process.

rows.notes: Varies by year, condition, grade, mileage, and spec.
Freight and marine insurance from Japan to NZ.
These are part of the import process once the car arrives in NZ.

auction_checklist.why: Gives a first view of how the car has been presented at auction.
Performance models can have changes that affect suitability for NZ entry and ownership.
Helps confirm wear, trim condition, and whether the spec matches the listing.
Useful for understanding how the car has been maintained.

not_for_you_block.headline: Who this may not suit
items: If you want the lowest possible running costs, a performance wagon like the S4 Avant B9 may not be the right direction.
If you need a high-riding SUV shape or seven seats, this wagon layout probably won't suit your day-to-day use.
If you only want to buy a car you can inspect locally today, importing from Japan may feel too slow.

alternatives_block.intro: If the S4 Avant B9 is close but not quite right, these are worth considering from the Japanese or NZ market.

rows.better_if: You want the wagon shape and premium feel without stepping into an S model.
rows.tradeoff: Less performance focus and less of the S4's character.

rows.better_if: You want a practical turbo wagon with strong everyday usability.
rows.tradeoff: Different cabin feel and badge appeal compared with the Audi.

rows.better_if: You want a smaller performance wagon with a more compact footprint.
rows.tradeoff: Less cargo space than the S4 Avant.

engine_drivetrain_block.headline: Powertrain and drivetrain points to check
engine_drivetrain_block.body: Because the source data does not include exact Japanese-market powertrain figures for this page, we don't publish guessed numbers. For each shortlisted S4 Avant B9, we confirm the drivetrain details, check for service history where available, and look closely at auction notes for modifications or mechanical issues that could affect ownership in NZ.
checklist: Confirm the exact Japanese-market powertrain and transmission details on the auction sheet
Check for service records or maintenance stickers where available
Review auction notes for warning lights, leaks, smoke, or driveline issues
Look for suspension, wheel, exhaust, or intake modifications on performance examples

hero.headline: Import an Audi S4 Avant B9
hero.body: A fast wagon with everyday space, sourced from Japanese auctions with clear paperwork and NZ-based support.

conditional-1.headline: Wagon space without giving up pace
conditional-1.body: The S4 Avant B9 appeals to buyers who want one car for commuting, family duties, and weekend trips. Japanese auction stock can give you more choice on condition, colour, and spec than waiting for the local market.
items: Useful rear cargo area for luggage, tools, or sports gear
Five-door wagon layout makes daily use easier than a sedan
Performance-focused drivetrain with practical body style
